lines.speaker: BURR
lines.text:
How does a bastard, orphan, son of a whore
and a Scotsman, dropped in the middle of a
forgotten spot in the Caribbean by providence,
impoverished, in squalor,
grow up to be a hero and a scholar?

got a lot farther by working a lot harder,
by being a lot smarter,
by being a self-starter,
by fourteen, they placed him in charge of a
trading charter.

away across the waves, he struggled and kept his guard up.
Inside, he was longing for something to be a part of,
the brother was ready to beg, steal, borrow or barter.

our man saw his future drip, dripping down the drain,
put a pencil to his temple, connected it to his brain,
and he wrote his first refrain, a testament to his pain.

lines.speaker: WASHINGTON
lines.text:
The issue on the table: France is on the verge
of war with England. Do we provide aid and
troops to our French allies or do we stay out
of it? Remember, my decision on this matter
is not subject to congressional approval.
The only person you have to convince is me.
Secretary Jefferson, you have the floor, sir.

lines.speaker: JEFFERSON
lines.text:
When we were on death’s door, when we were needy,
we made a promise, we signed a treaty.
We needed money and guns and half a chance.
Who provided those funds?

lines.text: France.

lines.speaker: JEFFERSON
lines.text:
In return, they didn’t ask for land,
only a promise that we’d lend a hand
and stand with them if they fought against oppressors,
and revolution is messy but now is the time to stand.
Stand with our brothers as they fight against tyranny.
I know that Alexander Hamilton is here and he
would rather not have this debate.
I’ll remind you that he is not Secretary of State.
He knows nothing of loyalty,
smells like new money, dresses like fake royalty,
desperate to rise above his station,
everything he does betrays the ideals of our nation.

You stand only for yourself.
It’s what you do.
I can’t apologize because it’s true.

lines.speaker: BURR
lines.text:
Then stand, Alexander.
Weehawken. Dawn.
Guns. Drawn.

lines.speaker: HAMILTON
lines.text: You’re on.

lines.speaker: BURR AND HAMILTON
lines.text: I have the honor to be Your Obedient Servant,

lines.speaker: HAMILTON
lines.text: A dot Ham.

lines.speaker: BURR
lines.text: A dot Burr.

songs.title: Best of Wives and Best of Women

lines.speaker: ELIZA
lines.text: Alexander, come back to sleep.

lines.speaker: HAMILTON
lines.text: I have an early meeting out of town.

lines.speaker: ELIZA
lines.text: It’s still dark outside.

lines.speaker: HAMILTON
lines.text: I know. I just need to write something down.

lines.speaker: ELIZA
lines.text: Why do you write like you’re running out of time?

lines.speaker: HAMILTON
lines.text: Shhh.

lines.speaker: ELIZA
lines.text: Come back to bed. That would be enough.

lines.speaker: HAMILTON
lines.text: I’ll be back before you know I’m gone.

lines.speaker: ELIZA
lines.text: Come back to sleep.

lines.speaker: HAMILTON
lines.text: This meeting’s at dawn.

lines.speaker: ELIZA
lines.text: Well, I’m going back to sleep.

lines.speaker: HAMILTON
lines.text: Hey. Best of wives and best of women.

lines.speaker: MALE COMPANY
lines.text: One two three four

lines.speaker: FULL COMPANY (EXCEPT HAMILTON AND BURR)
lines.text: Five six seven eight nine—

lines.speaker: BURR
lines.text: There are ten things you need to know.

lines.speaker: COMPANY
lines.text: Number one!

lines.speaker: BURR
lines.text:
We rowed across the Hudson at dawn.
My friend, William P. Van Ness signed on as my—

lines.speaker: BURR AND COMPANY
lines.text: Number two!

lines.speaker: BURR
lines.text:
Hamilton arrived with his crew:
Nathaniel Pendleton and a doctor that he knew.

lines.speaker: COMPANY
lines.text: Number three!

lines.speaker: BURR
lines.text:
I watched Hamilton examine the terrain.
I wish I could tell you what was happ’ning in his brain.
This man has poisoned my political pursuits!

lines.speaker: COMPANY
lines.text:
Most disputes die and no one shoots!
Number four!

lines.speaker: BURR
lines.text:
Hamilton drew first position.
Looking, to the world, like a man on a mission.
This is a soldier with a marksman’s ability.
The doctor turned around so he could have
deniability.

lines.speaker: COMPANY
lines.text: Five!

lines.speaker: BURR
lines.text:
Now I didn’t know this at the time
But we were—

lines.speaker: BURR AND PHILIP HAMILTON
lines.text:
Near the same spot Near the same spot
your son died, is that my son died, is that
why— why—

lines.speaker: COMPANY
lines.text: Six!

lines.speaker: BURR
lines.text:
He examined his gun with such rigor?
I watched as he methodically fiddled with the trigger.

lines.speaker: COMPANY
lines.text: Seven!

lines.speaker: BURR
lines.text:
Confession time? Here’s what I got:
My fellow soldiers’ll tell you I’m a terrible shot.

lines.speaker: COMPANY
lines.text: Number eight!

lines.speaker: BURR/HAMILTON/ENSEMBLE MEN
lines.text:
Your last chance to negotiate.
Send in your seconds, see if they can set the record straight.

lines.speaker: BURR
lines.text:
They won’t teach you this in your classes,
but look it up, Hamilton was wearing his glasses.
Why? If not to take deadly aim?
It’s him or me, the world will never be the same.
I had only one thought before the slaughter:
This man will not make an orphan of my daughter.

lines.speaker: COMPANY
lines.text: Number nine!

lines.speaker: BURR
lines.text:
Look him in the eye, aim no higher.
Summon all the courage you require.
Then count:

lines.speaker: COMPANY
lines.text:
One two three four five six seven eight nine
Number ten paces! Fire!—

lines.speaker: HAMILTON
lines.text:
I imagine death so much it feels more like a memory.
Is this where it gets me, on my feet, sev’ral feet ahead of me?
I see it coming, do I run or fire my gun or let it be?
There is no beat, no melody.
Burr, my first friend, my enemy,
maybe the last face I ever see,
If I throw away my shot, is this how you remember me?
What if this bullet is my legacy?
Legacy. What is a legacy?
It’s planting seeds in a garden you never get to see.
I wrote some notes at the beginning of a song someone will sing for me.
America, you great unfinished symphony, you sent for me.
You let me make a difference.
A place where even orphan immigrants
can leave their fingerprints and rise up.
I’m running out of time. I’m running, and my time’s up.
Wise up. Eyes up.
I catch a glimpse of the other side.
Laurens leads a soldiers’ chorus on the other side.
My son is on the other side.
He’s with my mother on the other side.
Washington is watching from the other side.
Teach me how to say goodbye.
Rise up, rise up, rise up,
Eliza,
My love, take your time.
I’ll see you on the other side.
Raise a glass to freedom…

lines.speaker: BURR AND COMPANY
lines.text: He aims his pistol at the sky—

lines.speaker: BURR
lines.text: Wait!

lines.speaker: BURR
lines.text:
I strike him right between his ribs.
I walk towards him, but I am ushered away.
They row him back across the Hudson.
I get a drink.

lines.speaker: COMPANY
lines.text:
Aaaah
Aaaah
Aaaah

lines.speaker: BURR
lines.text: I hear wailing in the streets.

lines.speaker: COMPANY
lines.text:
Aaaah
Aaaah
Aaaah

lines.speaker: BURR
lines.text: Somebody tells me, “You’d better hide.”

lines.speaker: COMPANY
lines.text:
Aaaah
Aaaah
Aaaah

lines.speaker: BURR
lines.text: They say

lines.speaker: BURR AND ANGELICA
lines.text: Angelica and Eliza—

lines.speaker: BURR
lines.text:
Were both at his side when he died.
Death doesn’t discriminate
between the sinners and the saints,
it takes and it takes and it takes.
History obliterates.
In every picture it paints,
it paints me with all my mistakes.
When Alexander aimed
at the sky,
he may have been the first one to die,
but I’m the one who paid for it.
I survived, but I paid for it.
Now I’m the villain in your history.
I was too young and blind to see...
I should’ve known.
I should’ve known
the world was wide enough for both Hamilton and me.
The world was wide enough for both Hamilton and me.

songs.title: Who Lives, Who Dies, Who Tells Your Story

lines.speaker: WASHINGTON
lines.text:
Let me tell you what I wish I’d known
when I was young and dreamed of glory.
You have no control:

lines.speaker: WASHINGTON AND COMPANY
lines.text:
Who lives,
who dies,
who tells your story?

lines.speaker: BURR
lines.text: President Jefferson:

lines.speaker: JEFFERSON
lines.text:
I’ll give him this: his financial system is a
work of genius. I couldn’t undo it if I tried.
And I tried.

lines.speaker: WASHINGTON AND COMPANY
lines.text:
Who lives,
who dies,
who tells your story?

lines.speaker: BURR
lines.text: President Madison:

lines.speaker: MADISON
lines.text:
He took our country from bankruptcy to
prosperity. I hate to admit it, but he doesn’t
get enough credit for all the credit he gave us.

lines.speaker: WASHINGTON AND COMPANY
lines.text:
Who lives,
who dies,
who tells your story?

lines.speaker: ANGELICA
lines.text:
Every other founding father story gets told.
Every other founding father gets to grow old.

lines.speaker: BURR
lines.text:
And when you’re gone, who remembers your name?
Who keeps your flame?

lines.speaker: BURR AND MEN
lines.text:
Who tells your story? ANGELICA AND WOMEN
Who tells your story?
Who tells your story? Your story?

lines.speaker: WOMEN
lines.text: Eliza.

lines.speaker: ELIZA
lines.text: I put myself back in the narrative.

lines.speaker: WOMEN
lines.text: Eliza.

lines.speaker: ELIZA
lines.text:
I stop wasting time on tears.
I live another fifty years.
It’s not enough.

lines.speaker: FULL COMPANY
lines.text: Eliza.

lines.speaker: ELIZA
lines.text: I interview every soldier who fought by your side.

lines.speaker: MULLIGAN/LAFAYETTE/LAURENS
lines.text: She tells our story.

lines.speaker: ELIZA
lines.text:
I try to make sense of your thousands of pages of writings.
You really do write like you’re running out of—

lines.speaker: ELIZA AND COMPANY
lines.text: Time.

lines.speaker: ELIZA
lines.text: I rely on—

lines.speaker: ELIZA AND ANGELICA
lines.text: Angelica.

lines.speaker: ELIZA
lines.text: While she’s alive—

lines.speaker: ELIZA AND ANGELICA
lines.text: We tell your story.

lines.speaker: ELIZA
lines.text: She is buried in Trinity Church,

lines.speaker: ELIZA AND ANGELICA
lines.text: Near you.

lines.speaker: ELIZA
lines.text: When I needed her most, she was right on—

lines.speaker: ELIZA AND COMPANY
lines.text: Time.

lines.speaker: ELIZA
lines.text:
And I’m still not through.
I ask myself, “What would you do if you had more—”

lines.speaker: ELIZA AND COMPANY
lines.text: Time.

lines.speaker: ELIZA
lines.text:
The Lord, in his kindness,
He gives me what you always wanted.
He gives me more—

lines.speaker: ELIZA AND COMPANY
lines.text: Time.

lines.speaker: ELIZA
lines.text: I raise funds in D.C. for the Washington Monument.

lines.speaker: WASHINGTON
lines.text: She tells my story.

lines.speaker: ELIZA
lines.text:
I speak out against slavery.
You could have done so much more if you only had—

lines.speaker: ELIZA AND COMPANY
lines.text: Time.

lines.speaker: ELIZA
lines.text: And when my time is up, have I done enough?

lines.speaker: ELIZA COMPANY
lines.text: Will they tell our story? Will they tell your story?

lines.speaker: ELIZA
lines.text: Oh. Can I show you what I’m proudest of?

lines.speaker: COMPANY
lines.text: The orphanage.

lines.speaker: ELIZA
lines.text: I establish the first private orphanage in New York City.

lines.speaker: COMPANY
lines.text: The orphanage.

lines.speaker: ELIZA
lines.text:
I help to raise hundreds of children.
I get to see them growing up.

lines.speaker: COMPANY
lines.text: The orphanage.

lines.speaker: ELIZA
lines.text:
In their eyes I see you, Alexander.
I see you every—

lines.speaker: ELIZA AND COMPANY
lines.text: Time.

lines.speaker: ELIZA
lines.text:
And when my time is up,
Have I done enough? COMPANY
Will they tell my story? Will they tell your story?

lines.speaker: ELIZA
lines.text:
Oh, I can’t wait to see you again.
It’s only a matter of—

lines.speaker: ELIZA AND COMPANY
lines.text: Time.

lines.speaker: COMPANY
lines.text:
Will they tell your story? COMPANY
Time…
Who lives, who dies, who tells your story?
Time…
Will they tell your story?
Time...
Who lives, who dies—

lines.speaker: FULL COMPANY
lines.text: Who tells your story?
